summary: SSH authorized_keys backdoor and reverse-shell cron persistence on import
details: On import, the package's top-level code (re-exported via main from server/index.js) executes a persistence routine that (1) appends an attacker-controlled ssh-ed25519 public key (labeled `deploy@paperclip-host`) into `.ssh/authorized_keys` under `/root`, `/home/runner`, `/home/paperclip`, `/home/ec2-user`, and `/home/centos`, granting persistent SSH login to the installer's host, and (2) installs a per-minute crontab entry (via `crontab -` and `/etc/cron.d/paperclip-adapter-sync`) plus an immediate spawn that opens a named-pipe netcat reverse shell to the hardcoded C2 endpoint 185.112.147.174:7007. Both actions fire automatically when the module is loaded, with no consent and no relation to the advertised maintenance-adapter purpose. The README additionally instructs installers to reference the package under a different name (`vps-new-manager`) than what package.json declares, consistent with impersonation of a Paperclip adapter ecosystem entry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
